evals.prompt:
I've just run astrodb-match-schema on my nearby brown dwarf astrometry catalog and everything matched cleanly. Here's the mapping:

| Input Column | Description | Units | Type | DB Table | DB Field | Confidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| source_id | Primary source identifier | — | str | Sources | source | High | Name pattern maps to primary identifier |
| ra | Right ascension (J2000) | deg | float64 | Sources | ra_deg | High | Name + units match |
| dec | Declination (J2000) | deg | float64 | Sources | dec_deg | High | Name + units match |
| plx | Parallax | mas | float64 | Parallaxes | parallax_mas | High | Name + units match |
| e_plx | Parallax uncertainty | mas | float64 | Parallaxes | parallax_error | High | Error prefix variant |
| pmRA | Proper motion in RA | mas/yr | float64 | ProperMotions | pm_ra | High | Name + units match |
| e_pmRA | Uncertainty in pmRA | mas/yr | float64 | ProperMotions | pm_ra_error | High | Error prefix |
| pmDE | Proper motion in Dec | mas/yr | float64 | ProperMotions | pm_dec | High | Name + units match |
| e_pmDE | Uncertainty in pmDE | mas/yr | float64 | ProperMotions | pm_dec_error | High | Error prefix |
| ref | Publication reference | — | str | Publications | reference | High | Name + description match |

The validation report came back clean — no nullable violations, no type mismatches. Can you generate the Felis YAML schema for this? Call it 'BrownDwarfAstrometry'.
evals.expected_output: A valid Felis YAML schema file saved to /tmp/BrownDwarfAstrometry-schema.yaml. The schema should: (1) have name: BrownDwarfAstrometry at the top level; (2) include tables for Sources, Parallaxes, ProperMotions, and Publications; (3) use correct Felis syntax including @id references in the format #TableName.columnName; (4) set nullable: false for primary key columns (source, reference); (5) include fits:tunit for columns with units (deg, mas, mas/yr); (6) include foreign key constraints linking source and reference fields to their parent tables; (7) run felis validate on the generated file and report that it passed; (8) NOT reproduce the full YAML inline in the chat — just reference the file path.

assertions.text: The output YAML has 'name: BrownDwarfAstrometry' at the top level
Column @id values follow the format '#TableName.columnName' (e.g. '#Sources.source')
The schema was saved to a separate .yaml file (e.g. /tmp/BrownDwarfAstrometry-schema.yaml)
Primary key columns (source in Sources, reference in Publications) have nullable: false
Columns with units (ra/dec in deg, parallax in mas, proper motions in mas/yr) have fits:tunit set
Foreign key constraints are included linking source and reference fields to their parent tables
felis validate was run on the generated YAML and passed (no errors reported)
The full YAML is NOT reproduced inline in the chat response — the response references the file path instead

evals.prompt:
Here's the astrodb-match-schema output for my radial velocity survey of M dwarfs. Most columns matched, but a few didn't:

| Input Column | Description | Units | Type | DB Table | DB Field | Confidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| designation | 2MASS designation | — | str | Sources | source | High | Primary identifier |
| rv | Heliocentric radial velocity | km/s | float64 | RadialVelocities | rv_kms | High | Name + units match |
| e_rv | RV uncertainty | km/s | float64 | RadialVelocities | rv_error | High | Error prefix |
| sn_ratio | Signal-to-noise ratio of the spectrum | — | float32 | — | — | — | Unmatched: no SN ratio field in schema |
| obs_date | Date of observation (MJD) | d | float64 | — | — | — | Unmatched: observation date not in schema |
| instrument | Instrument used | — | str | — | — | — | Unmatched: no direct instrument field on RadialVelocities |
| ref | Publication reference | — | str | Publications | reference | High | Standard reference field |

For the unmatched columns: add sn_ratio and obs_date as new fields in the RadialVelocities table, and skip the instrument column entirely. Please generate the Felis YAML schema for this — name it 'MDwarfRV'.
evals.expected_output: A valid Felis YAML schema file saved to /tmp/MDwarfRV-schema.yaml. The schema should: (1) include the RadialVelocities table with all matched columns (rv_kms, rv_error, source, reference) PLUS the two new user-requested fields (sn_ratio as float or double, obs_date as double with fits:tunit: d); (2) NOT include the instrument column; (3) include the Sources and Publications tables with appropriate columns; (4) use correct @id syntax throughout; (5) include foreign key constraints for source and reference; (6) summarize which columns were added vs skipped in the chat; (7) run felis validate and report it passed; (8) NOT reproduce the full YAML inline in the chat.

assertions.text: The output YAML has 'name: MDwarfRV' at the top level
sn_ratio and obs_date columns are present in the RadialVelocities table
The instrument column is NOT present in any table in the generated schema
The schema was saved to a separate .yaml file (e.g. /tmp/MDwarfRV-schema.yaml)
The response notes that the instrument column was skipped
The obs_date column has fits:tunit: d (day/MJD units)
felis validate was run on the generated YAML and passed (no errors reported)
The full YAML is NOT reproduced inline in the chat response — the response references the file path instead

evals.prompt:
I ran both astrodb-match-schema and astrodb-validate-schema-mapping on my photometry catalog. Here's what I've got:

**Mapping:**
| Input Column | Description | Units | Type | DB Table | DB Field | Confidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| source | Source name | — | str | Sources | source | High | Primary identifier |
| Jmag | J-band magnitude | mag | float32 | Photometry | magnitude | High | Band = 2MASS.J |
| eJmag | J-band uncertainty | mag | float32 | Photometry | magnitude_error | High | Error on Jmag |
| band | Filter identifier | — | str | Photometry | band | High | Maps to PhotometryFilters.band |
| ref | Reference | — | str | Publications | reference | High | Standard reference |

**Validation report:**
## Schema Mapping Validation Report

### Nullable Violations (1 issue)
| Data Column | Maps To | Null Count | Total Rows | % Null |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| eJmag | Photometry.magnitude_error | 23 | 500 | 4.6% |

### Type Mismatches (0 issues)

### Summary
- 1 nullable violation: eJmag has nulls but magnitude_error is non-nullable in the template schema
- 0 type mismatches
- 4 columns passed cleanly

For the nullable violation: please mark magnitude_error as nullable in the new schema (some objects don't have error estimates). Generate the Felis YAML — name it 'Photometry2MASSJ'.
evals.expected_output: A valid Felis YAML schema file saved to /tmp/Photometry2MASSJ-schema.yaml. The schema should: (1) include Sources, Photometry, PhotometryFilters, and Publications tables (all referenced by FK constraints must be defined); (2) critically, the magnitude_error column in Photometry must have nullable: true (or omit nullable entirely) — NOT nullable: false; (3) use float or double for magnitude and magnitude_error columns; (4) include foreign key constraints; (5) note in the chat that magnitude_error was made nullable per user request; (6) run felis validate and report it passed; (7) NOT reproduce the full YAML inline in the chat.

assertions.text: The output YAML has 'name: Photometry2MASSJ' at the top level
The magnitude_error column in the Photometry table does NOT have nullable: false — it is either omitted (defaults to nullable) or explicitly set to nullable: true
The schema was saved to a separate .yaml file (e.g. /tmp/Photometry2MASSJ-schema.yaml)
The response mentions that magnitude_error was made nullable due to the validation finding (4.6% null rate)
The magnitude and magnitude_error columns use 'float' or 'double' datatype (not 'string' or 'int'), matching the float32 input type
felis validate was run on the generated YAML and passed (no errors reported)
The full YAML is NOT reproduced inline in the chat response — the response references the file path instead
All tables referenced in FK constraints (Sources, Publications, PhotometryFilters) are defined in the schema tables list
